title: Part 1: Single-turn vs multi-turn, the eval that passes but ships a broken agent

cues.label: The puzzle
cues.headline: Green score, broken agent
cues.narration: The model aces every single-turn question, then falls over the moment it runs as an agent.

cues.label: What single-turn measures
cues.headline: One prompt, one graded response
cues.narration: A single-turn eval grades one response in isolation, with everything the model needs already present.

cues.label: The turn chain
cues.headline: A tool call spans turns
cues.narration: The agent requests a tool on one turn, gets the result on the next, and must reuse it on a later turn.

cues.label: The research grounding
cues.headline: BFCL V3 checks state across turns
cues.narration: The Berkeley leaderboard added multi-turn categories, while the FunctionGemma docs admit multi-turn workflows are not supported.

cues.label: The org root cause
cues.headline: No one owned the eval
cues.narration: Two teams disagreed over whether multi-turn was in scope, so the multi-turn eval had no owner and the gap shipped.
